## Heterogeneous Aggregations Required

Order No. 2222 **required each RTO/ISO to revise its tariff to allow different types of distributed energy resource technologies to part...

judge_notes.reason: The chunk explicitly states that 'the Commission required each RTO/ISO to revise its tariff to allow different types of distributed energy resource technologies to participate in a single distributed energy resource aggregation (i.e., allow heterogeneous distributed energy resource aggregations)'.
The chunk explicitly states 'We find that the benefits of allowing heterogeneous aggregations outweigh the concerns regarding complexity of implementation.'

The chunk explicitly states 'the inclusion of demand response resources in a heterogeneous distributed energy resource aggregation can allow the aggregation to collectively deliver ancillary services that those resources would not otherwise be able to provide.'

The chunk references footnote 76 citing Exelon Comments explaining that pairing a summer-only demand response resource with wind enables meeting reliability needs, which substantively supports the claim about complementary seasonal characteristics satisfying reliability needs.

The chunk states that 'aggregations made up solely of resources that participate as demand response resources' continue to be subject to the Order No. 719 opt-out, meaning RERRAs retain authority to exclude them, which supports the claim.

The chunk explicitly states that the opt-out applies to 'aggregations made up solely of resources that participate as demand response resources' and declines to extend this opt-out to heterogeneous aggregations 'made up of different types of resources including demand response as opposed to those made up solely of demand response,' supporting the claim that heterogeneous aggregations are not solely aggregations of retail customers.

The chunk explicitly states 'We are unconvinced by arguments in favor of homogeneous aggregations' and that 'concerns about RTOs'/ISOs' ability to manage a diverse set of distributed energy resources are misplaced because the distributed energy resource aggregator, not an individual distributed energy resource in the aggregation, is the market participant with whom the RTO/ISO would be interacting.'
